L.sop_ref: EPA/Ricardo motoring curve at designed idle RPM. Motoring torque (Nm) × omega (rad/s) at idle = 24.0 Nm × 71.21 rad/s ≈ 1710 W. Substrate-native unit: W.
L.cessation_trace_ref: data/camry-2.4l-2az-fe.json#motoring_curve — six-point motoring torque curve, friction saturating at -30.05 Nm above ~1765 RPM. Idle-RPM value interpolated.

G_0.sop_ref: EPA/Ricardo BSFC map evaluated at (idle_RPM=680, brake_torque=0). Fuel rate × LHV: 0.038e-3 kg/s × 44 MJ/kg ≈ 1710 W. Substrate-native unit: W. At idle, brake_power = 0 by definition, so G_0 = L (chit = 0 exactly).
G_0.extrapolation_evidence_ref: data/camry-2.4l-2az-fe.json#fuel_map — bilinear interpolation of fuel_rate_gps grid at (rpm=680, torque=0).

tau_obs_canonical.sop_ref: Representative averaging window for BSFC steady-state measurement. Steady-state operating points are measured over ~10-30 seconds per cell in standard dyno protocols (SAE 2018-01-1412); the canonical observer averaging window is well above one combustion cycle (~10-60 ms) and well below thermal soak time (~minutes).
tau_obs_canonical.sweep_evidence_ref: data/camry-2.4l-2az-fe.json#fuel_map — 36 RPM × 29 torque grid is the substrate-class characterization sweep. Each grid cell is a steady-state averaging window.

forward_residuals.note: Forward residual is the diff between this driver's canonical reading and a known-good canonical representation. For a hello-world with no prior characterization of this engine through mpa-atlas, no known-good reference exists. Round-trip discipline applies once a second driver is written for this substrate-class.

backward_residuals.note: Backward residual is the substrate-native data predicted by applying the reference realizer to this driver's canonical output, compared to the original substrate-native data. Same caveat as forward: requires a reference realizer. Deferred.

retirement_triggers.cessation_failure_rule: Measured motoring torque at idle RPM drifts more than 10% from the calibrated value of 24 Nm.
retirement_triggers.drive_excursion_rule: Measured fuel rate at idle (RPM=680, brake_torque=0) drifts more than 15% from the calibrated value of 0.038 g/s.
retirement_triggers.regime_drift_rule: Designed idle RPM drifts more than 100 RPM from the calibrated value of 680 RPM, or idle no longer satisfies chit ≈ 0 within tolerance.
retirement_triggers.substrate_native_drift_clock_ref: engine_hours_or_miles — engines drift with use. For a modelled engine (Ricardo simulation), drift is zero by construction; this trigger applies once we calibrate against a physical engine.

seal.calibration_authority: mpa-engine v0 hello-world (Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7, ronviers session 2026-05-11)
